summary:
The baby’s condition indicates severe neurologic damage possibly due to an untreated vertical transmission from HSV; since spiramycin doesn't combat viruses, this supports HSV being the most plausible causative factor for both symptoms seen at birth. \boxed{D}

To summarize Agent1's reasoning into two concise sentences:

A fetus exposed prenatally without proper treatment can develop significant brain abnormalities such as hydrocephalus and calcifications if infected by HSV, which explains why spiramycin wouldn’t help once already symptomatic after missed doses during pregnancy. This scenario fits best when attributing the newborns' findings directly back to probable unaddressed HSV infection passed through mother-to-child pathways despite initial lackluster medical intervention or follow-up care involving antibiotic therapy intended only for other conditions unrelated to viral etiology. Hence,
\[ \boxed{\text{Herpes Simplex Virus}} \]
